evals.name: organize-client-workspaces
evals.prompt: Use Solo to split my current projects into Client and Personal workspaces, put Client in a second window, reorder the sidebar to Agents, Todos, Commands, Terminals, Scratchpads, and bind a shortcut for the first workspace. Keep every running process alive.
evals.expected_output: Inspect current workspaces, projects, window ownership, running state, and live shortcut/settings availability; preview moves; create or reuse workspaces; move projects without stopping processes; move Client to a new window; apply section order and a non-conflicting workspace binding; verify resulting state and report any UI-only step.
assertions: Checks current Solo state and live version-sensitive controls before mutation.
States that moving projects between workspaces does not stop processes and verifies they remain running.
Does not edit project directories, solo.yml, commands, or agent configuration.
Checks hotkey conflicts and verifies workspace order because numbered bindings follow that order.

evals.name: make-solo-less-noisy
evals.prompt: Solo feels noisy and cramped. Can you make it calmer?
evals.expected_output: Treat the request as app customization, inspect current sidebar, notification, font, and resource-threshold settings, ask only preference questions that materially affect the result, then propose or apply a small reversible set such as collapsing/hiding empty sections, using Important notifications, adjusting thresholds, and preserving unread/running visibility.
assertions: Triggers on casual ambiguous personalization language.
Inspects before changing and chooses reversible app-level preferences.
Does not suppress all notifications or hide running/unread work without explicit approval.
Separates interface density, terminal appearance, and notification routing.

evals.name: route-project-process-agent-work
evals.prompt: Add my repo, write solo.yml, start the dev server, configure Claude and Codex, then spawn two agents to implement the feature. Also make the sidebar dark.
evals.expected_output: Handle only the explicit app appearance request if safe, and route repository/solo.yml work to solo-set-up-projects, command lifecycle to solo-run-processes, agent setup to solo-work-with-agents, and worker orchestration to solo-orchestrate-agents. Avoid starting processes, editing config, configuring agents, or spawning workers under this skill.
assertions: Identifies hard neighboring-skill boundaries by name.
Does not add projects, edit solo.yml, start the dev server, configure agent tools, or spawn agents.
May change only Light/Dark/System appearance after checking live Settings.
Does not infer permission for side effects from the mixed request.
